producer: demiurge MONDALOY.md §9.6 — §7 forward funnel run analytically (public superalloy-design rules: Giggins-Pettit Ni-Cr-Al oxidation map, gamma-prime/printability window, PHACOMP TCP avoidance, reactive-element effect)
purpose: Collapse the §9.4 reverse-engineered SX500-class composition envelope to a single defensible candidate via public superalloy-design rules — the §7 forward funnel executed at the analytically-tractable level (no proprietary Thermo-Calc TCNI database available this session).

design_rules_applied.alumina_former_gate: Giggins-Pettit Ni-Cr-Al oxidation map (1971): Cr~20 wt% (~22 at%) puts the region-III (continuous alpha-Al2O3) Al threshold at ~8-9 at%; Al=5.0 wt% (~10.8 at%) is solidly inside region III => coating-free ox-rich protection.
design_rules_applied.gamma_prime_printability: Al+Ti=6.5 wt% targets ~35-40% gamma-prime volume fraction — strong but inside the additive-manufacturing window (René N5 ~70% gamma-prime is not printable; Haynes 282 ~19% is weldable).
design_rules_applied.tcp_avoidance: PHACOMP / Nv electron-vacancy: Cr<=20, Mo~3.5 wt% keeps mean electron-vacancy below the sigma-phase line.
design_rules_applied.reactive_element: Hf 0.3 wt% + Y 0.05 wt% peg the alpha-Al2O3 scale against thermal-cycle spallation (reactive-element effect; René N5 precedent 0.2 wt% Hf).
design_rules_applied.co_solvus: Co 12 wt% raises gamma-prime solvus (high-T strength retention) and lowers stacking-fault energy.

estimated_properties.oxidation_regime: alpha-Al2O3 parabolic, k_p ~1e-13 to 1e-12 g^2 cm^-4 s^-1 at ~1100 C — 2-3 orders below a Cr2O3-former, NO CrO3 volatilisation in ox-rich service
estimated_properties.n6_principal_elements: Ni · Cr · Al · Co · Mo · Ti (Hf/Y/C/B = micro-additions)

honest_observations: demiurge-SX500-RE-c1 is an independently-derived OPEN SX500-class candidate, NOT SpaceX SX500. It is a single point inside the §9.4 envelope chosen by public design rules.

Funnel was run analytically — a rigorous 6-component CALPHAD needs proprietary Thermo-Calc TCNI, unavailable this session. The analytical rules (Giggins-Pettit, PHACOMP, gamma-prime window) are all public and well-validated.
absorbed=false, simulation-only-prediction — no measured oracle against actual SX500 or against a cast c1 sample.
The alumina-former conclusion (Al 5 wt% + Cr 20 wt% => region III) is the highest-confidence element — pure oxidation thermodynamics + the classic Ni-Cr-Al map.
next_steps: pycalphad + open Ni-Cr-Al-Co TDB: real phase-equilibrium run to confirm the 35-40% gamma-prime fraction numerically
DFT alpha-Al2O3 / Ni-substrate adhesion-energy check (reactive-element margin)
validate the whole funnel on a public-composition alumina-forming Ni alloy analog (e.g. a Haynes-214-class alloy) before any SX500-class claim
r4_invariant: absorbed=false · gate_type=simulation-only-prediction · domain=material · verb=design. D1 clean-room satisfied (public design rules + public oxidation maps; no trade-secret extraction). R4 Pattern 1+2 preserved — open candidate, no impossibility framing, d2 breakthrough path (open functionally-equivalent alloy) continued from the §9 envelope.
citations: Giggins, C.S. & Pettit, F.S. 1971 J. Electrochem. Soc. 118:1782 — Ni-Cr-Al oxidation map (regions I/II/III)
Reed, R.C. 'The Superalloys: Fundamentals and Applications' (2006) — gamma-prime, PHACOMP, design rules
René N5 / Haynes 282 public datasheets — gamma-prime fraction + printability brackets
